memoryArtifacts.headline: This memorial is dedicated to my ancestor William Montagu (alias Montacute). He was the 1st Earl of Salisbury and King of the Isle of Man an English nobleman and loyal servant of King Edward III.
memoryArtifacts.description:
Birth: 	1301
Cassington
Oxfordshire, England
Death: 	Jan. 30, 1344
Windsor
Berkshire, England

This memorial is dedicated to my ancestor William Montagu (alias Montacute). He was the 1st Earl of Salisbury and King of the Isle of Man an English nobleman and loyal servant of King Edward III. 
The son of William de Montagu and Elizabeth Montfort, the second Baron Montagu, he entered the royal household at an early age, and became a close companion to Prince Edward. The relationship continued after Edward was crowned king following the deposition of Edward II in 1327. In 1330, Montagu was one of Edward's main accomplices in the coup against Roger Mortimer, who until then had been acting as the king's protector.
In the following years Montagu served the king in various capacities, primarily in the Scottish Wars. He was richly rewarded, and among other things received the lordship of the Isle of Man. In 1337, he was created Earl of Salisbury, and given an annual income of 1000 marks to go with the title. He served in the early years of the Hundred Years' War.
William was born in Cassington in Oxfordshire in 1301. The Montagu family, a West Country family with roots going back to the Conquest, held extensive lands in Somerset, Dorset and Devon. 
Around 1327 William married Catherine,daughter of William de Grandison, 1st Baron Grandison. One anecdotal story revolve around Catherine Montagu; she is identified as the "Countess of Salisbury" from whose dropped garter Edward III named the Order of the Garter. William and Catherine had six children, most of whom made highly fortunate matches with other members of the nobility. 
Salisbury's oldest son William succeeded his father in July 1349, while still a minor, as William Montagu, 2nd Earl of Salisbury. The younger William was one of the founding members of the Order of the Garter, but he never enjoyed the same favor with the king as his father had.
*********************
Another son John De Montagu born 1327 in Somerset, England and died Feb 25 1390 in Salisbury Wiltshire, England. He married Margaret De Monthermer Jul 2 1340 in Stokeham, Devon, England, daughter of Thomas Monthermer and Margaret Tipoft. She was born Oct 14 1329 in Stokenham, Devon, England and died Mar 24 1305 in Cassington, Oxfordshire, England. (contributed by Amanda)


 
 
Family links:

  William Montagu (1285 - 1319)

Burial:

nameMeanings.name: Montagu
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016

variants.name: Montague
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from either Montaigu-le-bois (Manche) or Montaigu (Manche, Aisne), named with Old French mont ‘hill’ + agu ‘pointed’ (from Latin acutus). Drogo de Montagud was a companion of William the Conqueror and founder of the Montagu(e) family, among whom the personal name Drogo was revived in the 19th century. Another family of the name Monatgu have held the titles of Duke of Montagu, Earl of Sandwich, Earl and Duke of Manchester, Earl of Halifax, and Earl of Beaulieu, and.
Irish: adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Mac Taidhg (see McTigue ).

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.description: Capture of Castle Rushen by Robert the Bruce.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When William Montagu was born in March 1301, in Cassington, Oxfordshire, England, his father, William Montagu 2nd Baron Montagu, was 25 and his mother, Elizabeth De Montfort, was 29. He married Catherine Grandison in 1320, in Cassington, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 30 January 1344, in Windsor Castle, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 42, and was buried in Bisham, Berkshire, England, United Kingdom.

ja.lifeSummary: William Montaguが1301年3月にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアカシントンで生まれた時，父William Montagu 2nd Baron Montaguは25歳，母Elizabeth De Montfortは29歳であった。1320年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアカシントンでCatherine Grandisonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1344年1月30日にイギリスイギリスバークシュアイアウィンドソル カストルで42歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスバークシュアイアビシャムにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: William Montagu(이)가 1301년 3월 영국 스퍼드실르 캐징턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William Montagu 2nd Baron Montagu(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth De Montfort(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1320년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 캐징턴에서 Catherine Grandison(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1344년 1월 30일 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 윈드소르 카스틀에서 42세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 버크실르 비샤에 묻혔다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда William Montagu родился марта 1301 г. в местности кассингтон, оксфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени William Montagu 2nd Baron Montagu было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth De Montfort, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Catherine Grandison в 1320 г. в местности кассингтон, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери. Он умер 30 января 1344 г. в местности виндзор кастл, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 42 лет и был похоронен в местности бишам, биркшир, Англия, Великобритания.
